records.stage_evidence: Document describes completed procedure: 'C&D done' (closure and dressing).

ocr.verbatim:
Operation Notes
To be filled in by Surgeon
Date of Surgery 3/4/24
Start Time 4 pm
End Time 5 pm
Pre Operative Diagnosis Δ(R) Renal Calculus
Surgeon Dr. Anurag Gangcman
Anesthetist D. R. Mukab
Post Operative Diagnosis
Name of Procedure (R) PCNL + DJ stent
Type of Anesthesia GA
Assistants
Pediatrician
Operative Steps
RIGHT PCNL WITH DJ STENT
Patient is placed in lithotomy position. Position and draping done. Guide wire introduced in to right ureter and passed beyond calculi in pelvis.
Ureteric stent introduced over the guide wire and placed beyond calculus in kidney. Guide wire is removed. Patient is turned in prone position. Radio opaque dye is introduced in to kidney via ureteric catheter. Upper calyx posteriorly is identified on C ARM. Puncture needle introduced in to upper calyx. Tract dilated over guide wire 26 f amplatz sheath introduced into middle in to upper calyx. Calculi is fragmented and removed. 5 F DJ stent is placed antegradey. Puncture wound is closed. C&D done.

Operative Steps
RIGHT PCNL WITH DJ STENT
Patient is placed in lithotomy position. Position and draping done. Guide wire introduced in to right ureter and passed beyond calculi in pelvis.
Ureteric stent introduced over the guide wire and placed beyond calculus in kidney. Guide wire is removed. Patient is turned in prone position. Radio opaque dye is introduced in to kidney via ureteric catheter. Upper calyx posteriorly is identified on C ARM. Puncture needle introduced in to upper calyx. Tract dilated over guide wire 26 f amplatz sheath introduced into middle in to upper calyx. Calculi is fragmented and removed. 5 F DJ stent is placed antegradey. Puncture wound is closed. C&D done.

records.summary: This is a typed surgical note documenting a right PCNL (Percutaneous Nephrolithotomy) with DJ stent placement for renal calculi. The procedure was performed on April 3, 2024, by Dr. Anurag Gangcman under general anesthesia. The note details the steps: patient positioning, guide wire placement, stent insertion, dye injection, calyx identification, puncture, tract dilation, stone fragmentation, DJ stent placement, and wound closure.
key_observations: Procedure: Right PCNL with DJ stent for renal calculus
Operative Steps: Guide wire, stent, dye, puncture, dilation, stone removal, DJ stent placement, wound closure

records.stage_evidence: Report describes post-voiding film and final opinion after contrast study

ocr.verbatim:
Patient Name: 
Ref. By: 
Part Scanned IVP

INTRA VENOUS PYELOGRAPHY

KUB - Bilateral psoas shadows are normal
B/L renal shadows are normal
Two radio opaque shadows seen in right renal s/o calculus

IVP done with 80 c.c of contrast (Double Contrast) and film were taken at
5 min, 15 min, 45min with pre and post voiding.

15-45 min. FILM * Right side nephrogram & pylogram seen. normally with normal right ureter seen

45---Min film Left side nephrogram & pylogram seen. normally with normal left ureter Up to lower end

FULL BLADDER Normal in size, shape and contour.
No calculus seen.
No diverticula are seen.

POST VOID FILM * Post voiding film shows no significant amount of urine.

OPINION:- 1- FINDINGS ARE SUGGESTIVE OF B/L NORMAL EXCERETORY KIDNEY
2- -- TWO CALCULUS LARGEST OF 12 MM SEEN IN LOWER CALYX OF RIGHT KIDNEY .

(Radiodiagnosis )
UKMC Reg no 16250

(Radiodiagnosis )
UKMC Reg No 1721

NOT FOR MEDICO LEGAL PURPOSE

records.summary: This is a typed radiology report for an Intravenous Pyelography (IVP) performed on a 55-year-old female patient. The report details findings from multiple films taken at different time intervals after contrast administration, concluding with an opinion that the kidneys are normal except for two calculi in the right kidney, the largest being 12 mm in the lower calyx.
key_observations: Procedure: IVP with 80 c.c. double contrast
Findings: Normal bilateral renal shadows, two radio-opaque shadows in right kidney suggestive of calculi
Opinion: Bilateral normal excretory kidneys with two calculi (largest 12 mm) in lower calyx of right kidney
Post-voiding film shows no significant urine

records.stage_evidence: IVP contrast medium visible in urinary tract, multiple views of kidneys, ureters, and bladder
records.summary: A set of four IVP (Intravenous Pyelogram) radiographs showing the pelvicalyceal system with contrast medium outlining the kidneys, ureters, and bladder. The images are displayed on a digital radiography viewer with patient and technical parameters visible.
key_observations: Contrast medium is visible within the urinary tract structures.
Multiple views (AP, lateral, oblique) of the abdomen and pelvis are shown.
Patient identifier and date (23-Mar-2026) are visible at the top.
Image quality appears good with clear anatomical detail.

records.stage_evidence: No contrast medium visible; no catheter or stent in place; appears to be a diagnostic study
records.summary: A posteroanterior (PA) abdominal X-ray showing the pelvicalyceal system with a catheter in place, likely for a diagnostic or therapeutic procedure. The patient is a 55-year-old female. The image is dated April 5, 2026.
key_observations: A catheter is visible extending from the lower abdomen into the pelvis.
Spine and pelvic bones are clearly visible.
Image is labeled as Digital Radiography.

limitations: Image is a photograph of a monitor, not the original film or digital image.
Partial view of the image frame visible on the left side.
